tickets.title: Author D2 characterization rails (fresh context, pre-restructure)
tickets.body:
Motivation: T1-T4 restructure src/identify.js's D2 loop. Pin what must
not change *before* anyone changes it (ADLC P3; rails authored fresh-context,
frozen). Author in a fresh context from do-better/SKILL.md's D2 section plus
the public contracts documented in handoff.md §2 -- NOT by reading
src/identify.js's internals.

Scope: test/identify-rails.test.js (new file only).

Pin (bug-compatible where noted):
1. run(ctx) without a passed comprehend gate throws OpError (exit 1).
2. Gate pass requires all dimensions dry AND zero unverified written; gate
   fail throws GateError with .gate === "identify", .state attached, exit 2.
3. Verified findings are written via writeFinding with status: "verified",
   evidence [{file, line, sha}], and a reproduction record; killed
   candidates are never written.
4. Re-run seeds dedupe from readFindings -- no duplicate finding files or
   IDs (D6 idempotency).
5. Offline mode: deterministic static pass per dimension, single pass,
   declared degradation in the summary string.
6. Candidate validation drops unsafe paths / bad severities silently (fail
   closed).
7. State shape: phases.identify.{passesByDimension, killed, verified} and
   gates.identify.{passed, dryPassesByDimension, unverified} keys exist
   (T1 may ADD keys; these must remain).

Explicit non-pins -- do NOT assert these; T1-T4 legitimately change them:
pass-count magnitudes, exact GateError detail wording, or any LLM
system/user prompt content. Assert presence/shape/invariants only (e.g.
"passesByDimension[dim] is a positive integer"), never today's specific
numbers or strings. A rail that pins these makes T1 unlandable for a defect
authored into the plan, not the code.

Acceptance criteria:
- AC1: node --test test/identify-rails.test.js green against current main,
  before any T1 code lands. (Verification: run on main.)
- AC2: No existing test file modified. (Verification: git diff --stat main
  -- test/ shows only the new file.)
- AC3: Rails are non-vacuous. CORRECTION FROM EXECUTION: adlc hollow-test
  cannot validate this -- it unconditionally excludes test/spec files from
  mutation targets and is strictly diff-scoped with no explicit-target flag;
  T0's diff is test-only, so it always reports 0 mutants/exit 0 regardless
  of rail quality. Use a manual regression-injection spot-check instead:
  for each pin, temporarily break the corresponding behavior in
  src/identify.js, confirm the pinned rail goes red, then revert. Spot-check
  found a real gap in the first draft of pin 1 (an adjacent guard clause
  produced an indistinguishable OpError under the non-pin rule) -- fixed by
  isolating the precondition. (Manual mechanical check, documented in the
  commit message.)

Full context: handoff.md §3 "T0", §2 "Ground rules", §2b (mechanical gates).

tickets.title: Coverage-driven dry loop + packet-starvation fix (D2 identify)
tickets.body:
Motivation: finder passes today see <=30KB (PACKET_BYTES) of a
~150KB deep-read set, with a break-not-skip bug in buildFinderPacket that can
make the gate pass with zero findings on an empty packet, silently. Fix
converts the loop from "rotate a shared window" to "partition the whole
deep-read set into packets; every (dimension x packet) cell runs
loop-until-dry independently."

Scope: src/identify.js, do-better/SKILL.md (D2 section),
do-better/references/verification.md (coverage note), README.md,
test/identify.test.js (additions), test/identify-coverage.test.js (new).

Design (decided -- do not relitigate):
1. Fix buildFinderPacket: replace break with skip-and-continue; a single
   chunk exceeding PACKET_BYTES is hard-truncated via existing truncate()
   rather than dropped. Given >=1 readable slice, the packet is never empty.
2. New pure function partitionSlices(slices, maxBytes = PACKET_BYTES)
   returns [{files: [names], packet: string}] -- every readable slice in
   exactly one packet; oversized slices become singleton truncated packets;
   deterministic input-order.
3. Loop inversion: for each dimension (descending charter weight), for each
   packet, loop-until-dry (K_DRY=2 consecutive zero-new passes, cap
   MAX_PASSES=8 per dimension x packet cell). rotate() is deleted.
   Prior-conclusions list stays pool-wide per dimension (not per-packet).
4. Starvation is a gate failure, never silent: empty/unreadable deep-read
   set online throws GateError (.gate="identify"); offline is exempt.
5. State additions: phases.identify.packetsByDimension {dimId:count};
   passesByDimension becomes total passes summed across packets;
   gates.identify gains packetsByDimension; not-dry detail names dimension
   AND packet index.
6. D2 finder coverage manifest: after the loop, idempotently write a
   "## D2 finder coverage" section in
   .dobetter/comprehension/coverage-manifest.md: per dimension -- files
   examined, packet count, total passes, truncated-slice list; unreadable
   deep-read files under a "### Unexamined" subheading. Offline: section is
   still written (never empty) -- files examined from T0 rail 5's static
   pass; packet count/passes are 0/1 with a "(offline -- no packetization)"
   note.
7. Cost note in README + SKILL.md: calls scale as
   dims x packets x passes x poolN; --budget remains the hard ceiling.
8. Persisted per-cell dry state (in scope, not deferred):
   phases.identify.dryCellsByDimension {dimId:[packetIndex,...]} records
   packets that reached K_DRY. Re-run against the SAME headSha skips
   already-dry packets entirely (zero re-issued finder calls). A SHA change
   discards all dry-cell state. Makes BudgetError resumable instead of
   restart-from-zero.

Acceptance criteria (each names its verification method):
- AC1: partitionSlices covers all slices exactly once; oversized slice ->
  singleton truncated packet; never returns [] for non-empty input. (Unit
  test, test/identify-coverage.test.js.)
- AC2: Regression test reproducing the break-bug -- a slice whose numbered
  form exceeds PACKET_BYTES at rotation head -- fails on main's builder,
  passes on the new one; fake-LLM finder receives the OTHER file's content
  too (assert via fake-LLM prompt log). (Integration test, fake-LLM seam.)
- AC3: With 2 dimensions x 3 packets in the fake fixture, every packet's
  content appears in >=1 finder prompt per dimension. (Integration test.)
- AC4: Empty/unreadable deep-read set online -> GateError exit 2 with
  descriptive detail; offline unaffected. (Integration test.)
- AC5: coverage-manifest.md gains the "## D2 finder coverage" section;
  re-running replaces rather than duplicates it. (Integration test.)
- AC6: T0 rails + full existing suite green. (node --test.)
- AC7: SKILL.md D2 step 1-2 and README updated to describe packetized
  coverage. (Reviewer check -- grep for "packet" in both.)
- AC8: BudgetError mid-run, same-SHA re-run: cells already dry are not
  re-queried (zero fake-LLM calls for those cells); a not-yet-dry cell
  resumes from its recorded pool. SHA change clears all dry-cell state.
  (Integration test.)
- AC9: README gains a "Sizing --budget" table with expected D2 call counts
  for a reference repo shape (8 dims x ~5 packets x K_DRY=2 x
  charter-weighted N -- see T2). (Reviewer check.)

Full context: handoff.md §3 "T1", §5 (partitionSlices contract), §7 (cost
risk).

tickets.title: Pooled, lens-diverse D2 finders (fan width)
tickets.body:
Motivation: finder passes today are near-identical (same model,
same prompt, slices merely rotated) -- "dry" can mean "the same context
converged," not "the codebase is exhausted." Fixes by fanning N finders per
pass with distinct lenses (E1 sampling diversity, P5 pooled prosecutors).

Scope: src/identify.js, do-better/references/refute-charter.md (lens
section), src/utils.js (only if --n flag plumbing needs it -- it already
parses), do-better/SKILL.md, README.md (flags table),
test/identify-pool.test.js (new).

Design (decided):
1. Reuse the existing --n flag (D1 parallax width, default 3) as the
   MAXIMUM D2 pool width. Charter-weighted (in scope, not deferred):
   effective width = min(--n, charterPoolWidth(weight)) where weight>=4 ->
   full --n, weight 2-3 -> max(1, floor(--n/2)), weight 1 -> 1 (no pooling,
   identical to v0.1 for that dimension). Document the table in both flag
   tables and references/taxonomy.md.
2. Add a "## Lenses" section to references/refute-charter.md with 5 named
   lenses (one paragraph each): exploit-author, oncall-3am, new-hire-reader,
   performance-profiler, staff-skeptic. LEAKAGE HAZARD (must fix, found in
   review): the finder system prompt (refuteSystem, identify.js:425,
   loadRef("refute-charter.md", FALLBACK_REFUTE)) is loaded WHOLESALE today
   and passed verbatim to every finder -- left as-is, every finder would see
   ALL 5 lens descriptions, diluting the diversity this ticket adds. Fix: a
   new pure parseLenses(refuteDoc) returns {base: string, lenses:
   [{id,text}]} -- base is everything ABOVE the "## Lenses" heading (used as
   refuteSystem, replacing today's whole-file load); lenses is the parsed
   catalog. Fallback (section absent/unparseable): base = whole doc
   (today's behavior preserved), lenses = hardcoded 5-entry const array,
   degrade loudly via log.warn.
3. Each pass of a (dimension x packet) cell issues N finder calls (N =
   charter-weighted width); call i's system prompt is base + "\n\nLens: " +
   lenses[(passIndex+i) % lenses.length].text -- finder sees ONLY its
   assigned lens, never the catalog.
4. Pool admission: candidates from all N calls validated+deduped together;
   newCount is the pooled total; dry streak advances only when the POOL
   yields zero new. passesByDimension still counts passes, not calls.
5. A single finder-call failure inside a pool: BudgetError rethrows
   (stop-the-world); any other OpError after the LLM layer's own 3 retries
   fails the phase (fail closed).

Acceptance criteria:
- AC1: With --n 3, each pass issues exactly 3 finder calls with 3 distinct
  lens strings (assert on fake-LLM prompt log). (Integration test.)
- AC2: Dry streak: pool pass A yields {2 new,0,0} -> not dry; passes B and C
  all-zero -> dry at K=2. (Integration test, scripted fake.)
- AC3: Lens parsing: doc-driven lenses used when present; hardcoded fallback
  + warning when section missing. (Unit test.)
- AC4: --n 1 reproduces pre-T2 single-finder call counts. (Integration
  test.)
- AC5: T0 rails + full suite green; SKILL.md/README flag tables updated.
- AC6: Charter-weighted width: weight-1 dimension issues exactly 1 finder
  call per pass regardless of --n; weight-5 issues --n calls; weight-3
  issues max(1, floor(--n/2)). (Unit + integration test.)
- AC7: A finder's system prompt contains its assigned lens text and does
  NOT contain the "## Lenses" heading or any other lens's text (assert on
  fake-LLM system-prompt log -- the leakage regression item 2 exists to
  prevent). (Integration test.)

Full context: handoff.md §3 "T2".

tickets.title: Semantic dedupe for D2 candidates
tickets.body:
Motivation: dedupe key today is sha256(dimension|file|normalized
claim) -- verbatim wording. Models rephrase; real duplicates re-enter the
pool (dry streak resets on noise). Adds a cheap-tier semantic equivalence
check layered over the existing hash filter.

Scope: src/identify.js, test/identify-dedupe.test.js (new),
do-better/SKILL.md (D2 step 2 wording).

Design (decided):
1. Layered admission for each validated candidate:
   a. Hash filter (existing dedupeKey) -- free, unchanged, still exported.
   b. Semantic filter (hash-survivors only, online only): one cheap-tier
      jsonCall per candidate comparing against prior admitted entries with
      the SAME dimension AND SAME file (small list): prompt lists prior
      {index,title,claim}, asks {"duplicateOf": null | <index>}. Duplicate
      -> not admitted, does not reset the dry streak, and its key is added
      to seen so the paraphrase is not re-litigated next pass.
2. Fails OPEN, deliberately: unparseable/erroring semantic check => treat
   as new. Rationale: a false-new costs one wasted verification; a
   false-duplicate loses a finding permanently -- verification downstream
   kills junk, nothing resurrects a wrongly-suppressed finding. This is the
   ONE sanctioned fail-open in D2 -- document it in a code comment AND
   SKILL.md.
3. Offline: hash-only (declared degradation in the summary line).
4. Prior-findings seeding (D6) also participates: semantic comparison list
   includes prior verified findings for that dimension+file (from
   readFindings).

Acceptance criteria:
- AC1: Paraphrase fixture -- same defect, different wording, same
  dimension+file -- is suppressed; dry streak advances; no second finding
  file on re-run. (Integration test, scripted fake returning duplicateOf.)
- AC2: Genuinely distinct claim in the same file is admitted. (Integration
  test.)
- AC3: Semantic-check failure => candidate admitted (fail open) +
  log.warn. (Integration test.)
- AC4: Semantic check runs at tier:"cheap" and only for same
  dimension+file pairs. (Integration test.)
- AC5: T0 rails + full suite green.

Full context: handoff.md §3 "T3".

tickets.title: Multi-citation claims + bounded NEED_CONTEXT verification
tickets.body:
Motivation: today's blind verifier is a single frontier vote over
one cited file +/-40 lines -- cross-file claims (architecture,
maintainability, operability) cannot be supported by one slice and are
correctly killed, systematically biasing surviving findings toward local
trivia. Fixes by letting findings cite multiple files and letting the
verifier request one bounded round of extra context, without breaking
verifier blindness (the finder's own reasoning is still withheld).

Scope: src/identify.js (finder prompt JSON shape, verifyCandidate,
codeSlice usage), src/artifacts.js (ADDITIVE ONLY: finding evidence may
carry >1 citation -- first confirm writeFinding/readFindings already
round-trip an evidence array; T0 rail 3 already pins evidence as an array,
so this is very likely a no-op edit), do-better/references/verification.md,
do-better/references/templates/finding-template.md, do-better/SKILL.md,
test/identify-crossfile.test.js (new).

Design (decided):
1. Candidate schema (additive): finders may return citations:
   [{file,line},...] (<=4). file/line remain required, treated as
   citations[0] when citations is absent. Validation: every citation passes
   isSafeRelPath + integer line>=1, else the WHOLE candidate is dropped
   (fail closed).
2. Deterministic stage: verifyCitations must verify ALL citations; any
   failure kills the candidate.
3. Repro stage: unchanged.
4. Blind reread stage: verdict prompt includes one +/-40-line slice per
   citation (cap 4 slices; total slice budget 24,000 chars via truncate).
   Verifier remains blind to proposer reasoning.
5. NEED_CONTEXT round (exactly one): VERDICT_SYSTEM gains a fourth verdict
   NEED_CONTEXT with {"files":["rel/path",...]} (<=2). If returned, re-issue
   the verdict call ONCE with those files' slices appended (each
   isSafeRelPath-checked, truncated to 8,000 chars; unsafe/unreadable
   requests are simply omitted). Second response must be
   CONFIRM/KILL/UNCERTAIN; a SECOND NEED_CONTEXT => killed (fail closed).
   Reproduction record notes context-round:1 and which files were supplied.
6. Finding output: evidence array carries all verified citations;
   finding-template.md documents multi-citation evidence.

Acceptance criteria:
- AC1: Cross-file fixture: candidate citing 2 files, verdict CONFIRM =>
  finding written with 2 evidence entries, both SHA-pinned. (Integration
  test.)
- AC2: One bad citation among several => killed before any LLM verdict
  call (zero verdict entries in fake-LLM log). (Integration test.)
- AC3: NEED_CONTEXT round: first verdict NEED_CONTEXT with a safe path =>
  exactly one follow-up verdict call whose prompt contains the requested
  file's content; CONFIRM => verified with context-round in the
  reproduction record. (Integration test.)
- AC4: Second consecutive NEED_CONTEXT => killed; unsafe requested path =>
  omitted from the follow-up, not fetched. (Integration tests.)
- AC5: Single-citation candidates take NO NEED_CONTEXT round against the
  scripted fixtures (a real model may legitimately request context even
  for one citation -- that is the feature working, not a regression); the
  CONFIRM/KILL/UNCERTAIN verdict CALL COUNT per candidate is unchanged from
  v0.1 (one call, or two only on an actual NEED_CONTEXT round). Note:
  VERDICT_SYSTEM itself changes for every candidate (design item 5) -- this
  AC does NOT claim byte-identical prompts, only unchanged call-count
  behavior on fixtures that don't trigger NEED_CONTEXT. T0 rails and
  pre-T4 verdict-path tests stay green. (Rails + full suite + new
  scripted-fake call-count assertion.)
- AC6: verification.md + finding-template.md + SKILL.md updated.

Full context: handoff.md §3 "T4", §5 (Verdict JSON contract).

tickets.title: Distill: verified findings -> suggested permanent controls
tickets.body:
Motivation: verified findings never convert into permanent cheap
controls today -- every engagement re-buys the same detections (ADLC P7 /
lesson-foundry doctrine). Adds a standalone "distill" phase that clusters
findings and proposes the cheapest permanent defense per cluster.

Scope: src/distill.js (new, self-contained: exports run(ctx) + PHASE_ID),
src/artifacts.js (LAYOUT addition), do-better/SKILL.md (new section),
README.md, test/distill.test.js (new). Does NOT touch src/utils.js,
bin/cli.js, or src/refresh.js -- CLI registration and the refresh
auto-invoke wiring are ticket T5b, not this one (two review findings
established that utils.js and refresh.js are single-writer-owned by
worktree 1, where T6/T7 also edit them; claiming them "disjoint" from
worktree 1 was false both times and guaranteed a merge conflict).

Design (decided):
1. New command "npx do-better distill" (invocable standalone -- the
   auto-invoke-at-end-of-refresh wiring is T5b's job; src/distill.js
   exports run(ctx) for T5b to call, but this ticket does not itself touch
   refresh.js). Requires >=1 verified finding; otherwise a clean no-op with
   a message (exit 0).
2. Clustering: group verified findings (current + all prior runs -- they
   persist in findings/) by dimension, then one cheap-tier jsonCall per
   dimension proposing clusters: {"clusters":[{"label":"...",
   "findingIds":["F-..."], "rationale":"..."}]}. Offline fallback: cluster
   by identical static check.type only (declared).
3. Control proposal: for each cluster with >=2 findings, one mid-tier call
   proposing the cheapest permanent defense, constrained to three shapes:
   {"type":"grep","pattern":"...","file":"..."} or
   {"type":"grep-repo","pattern":"..."} (static check runnable by
   runReproCheck / added to staticFinderPass heuristics);
   {"type":"lint","tool":"eslint|other","sketch":"..."} (human-readable rule
   sketch); {"type":"skill","suggestion":"..."} (routed to skill-mining when
   present, reuse src/adlc.js locate/degrade table -- read ctx.adlc.available,
   do not re-locate ad hoc).
4. Verification of controls: grep-shaped controls are executed against the
   current worktree via runReproCheck; a control that doesn't currently
   fire is still emitted but marked dormant:true (defends the future).
   Lint/skill shapes marked unverified-suggestion.
5. Artifacts: .dobetter/distilled/lessons.md (human) and
   .dobetter/distilled/controls.json (machine: {version:1, generatedAt,
   headSha, controls:[{id:"C-NNN", cluster, findingIds, control,
   status:"verified"|"dormant"|"unverified-suggestion"}]}). Idempotent:
   re-runs regenerate both from the full findings set; C-NNN ids
   stable-keyed by cluster-label hash.
6. State: phases.distill recorded via recordPhase/addSpend (add "distill"
   to PHASES in src/state.js -- confirm additive-safety with the state
   round-trip tests). No gate, no human approval.

Acceptance criteria:
- AC1: Fixture with 3 verified findings (2 same-class) => one cluster of 2,
  one control emitted; controls.json validates against the shape above.
  (Integration test, scripted fake.)
- AC2: Grep-shaped control that fires => status:"verified" with a repro
  record; non-firing => "dormant". (Integration test.)
- AC3: Zero findings => exit 0, friendly message, no artifacts. (CLI test.)
- AC4: Re-run is idempotent -- stable C-NNN ids, no duplicate sections.
  (Integration test.)
- AC5: Full suite green; README/SKILL.md document the command's behavior
  and artifacts. (CLI registration, the refresh auto-invoke wiring, and
  their tests are T5b's ACs, not this ticket's.)

Full context: handoff.md §3 "T5".

tickets.title: Wire distill into the CLI surface and into refresh
tickets.body:
Motivation: two review findings, same failure class, twice: T5's
original scope claimed src/utils.js (round 1) and separately src/refresh.js
(round 2) as disjoint from worktree 1 for parallel execution, while T6/T7
also edit both there -- a guaranteed conflict in HELP_TEXT (round 1) and in
refresh.js's eval-repro re-verification logic (round 2, T7 also edits
refresh.js). Single-writer rule resolves both: utils.js/bin/cli.js/
refresh.js are worktree-1-owned; this ticket does all three integrations
there, after BOTH T5's src/distill.js and T7's refresh.js changes have
merged to main -- so this is also where the two independent refresh.js
changes (T7's eval-repro gating, this ticket's distill auto-invoke) get a
single integration test proving they compose correctly.

Scope: src/utils.js (COMMANDS set + HELP_TEXT), bin/cli.js (new "distill"
dispatch case calling distill.run), src/refresh.js (import distill.run,
invoke non-fatally at the end of a refresh run -- warn on throw, never fail
refresh's own exit code), test/cli.test.js and test/refresh.test.js
(additions).

Design (decided):
1. Add "distill" to COMMANDS in src/utils.js.
2. Add a distill section to HELP_TEXT.
3. Add the dispatch case in bin/cli.js, mirroring the existing per-phase
   command wiring (same shape as scan/charter/etc.).
4. In refresh.js, import distill.run and call it non-fatally at the end of
   a refresh run: a thrown error is caught, warned via log.warn, and does
   not change refresh's own exit code or gate outcome.

Acceptance criteria:
- AC1: "npx do-better --help" lists distill with its one-line description.
  (Spawn test, cli.test.js-style.)
- AC2: "npx do-better distill" dispatches to src/distill.js's run(ctx).
  (Integration test.)
- AC3: refresh still exits per its own contract when distill.run throws
  (warn-only, exit code unaffected). (Integration test with a fake that
  errors on distill labels.)
- AC4: COMPOSITION TEST (the reason this ticket waits for both T5 and T7):
  a single refresh run with --eval-repro unset correctly (a) skips
  re-executing eval-shaped stored repros -- flagging them
  stale-unverifiable per T7 -- AND (b) still auto-invokes distill.run at
  the end of the same run. Both behaviors asserted in ONE integration test,
  proving the two independently-developed refresh.js changes don't
  silently break each other on merge. (Integration test.)
- AC5: Full suite green; T0 rails untouched.

Full context: handoff.md §3 "T5b", §4 (single-writer sequencing rationale).

tickets.title: Bounded concurrency for D2 finder pools and verification
tickets.body:
Motivation: all finder/verify LLM calls are strictly sequential
awaits today -- wall-clock cost only, not correctness. Adds a small
zero-dep concurrency helper and applies it to the pool/verify paths T2/T3
add.

Scope: src/utils.js (mapLimit), src/identify.js (apply), test/utils.test.js
(additions), test/identify.test.js (determinism assertion).

Design (decided):
1. export async function mapLimit(items, limit, fn) in src/utils.js --
   zero-dep, order-preserving results, first rejection propagates after
   in-flight tasks settle.
2. Apply to: (a) the N pooled finder calls within a pass (limit=N, <=4);
   (b) per-candidate verification within a dimension (limit 4). Dimensions
   and packets remain sequential (keeps spend ordering and logs readable).
3. Budget note (document, don't fix): checkBudget is check-then-record;
   under concurrency the ceiling can overshoot by at most (limit-1) x
   maxCallCost. Acceptable and documented in a code comment + README budget
   row. Spend accumulation itself is single-threaded-safe (no await between
   read and write in recordSpend).
4. Determinism: pool admission order = lens index order (stable), regardless
   of completion order -- sort results before admission.

Acceptance criteria:
- AC1: mapLimit unit tests: order preserved, limit respected (concurrent
  counter probe), rejection propagates. (Unit tests.)
- AC2: Identify results (finding ids, files, state) byte-identical between
  limit=1 and limit=4 runs on the same scripted fake. (Integration test.)
- AC3: Full suite green.

Note: this ticket functionally depends only on T2+T3 (the pool/verify paths
it applies concurrency to) -- it has NO functional dependency on T4's
changes, even though it is sequenced after T4 in the single-writer worktree
order documented in handoff.md §4. That worktree-ordering constraint is
operational (avoiding concurrent-worktree conflicts on src/identify.js), not
a DAG dependency -- stated explicitly here so a coldstart agent does not
infer a missing functional dependency on T4.

Full context: handoff.md §3 "T6", §4 (sequencing rationale).

tickets.title: Eval-repro opt-in (--eval-repro flag, safe default)
tickets.body:
Motivation: sanitizeRepro today permits model-proposed "node -e"
snippets (<=500 chars) executed with full user privileges against the
target repo -- a hostile brownfield repo is a prompt-injection surface into
the mid-tier model proposing the snippet. The plan's own review called this
"unacceptable posture for enterprise engagements." DECISION (supersedes an
earlier opt-out design considered during planning): the default flips to
safe -- eval-repro is OFF by default, opt-in via a flag, not opt-out.

Scope: src/utils.js (parseArgs + HELP_TEXT), src/identify.js (sanitizeRepro
gains an options arg), src/refresh.js (re-verification must honor it for
stored eval-shaped repros), do-better/SKILL.md + README.md flag tables,
SPEC.md addendum note (T8's job) recording the default-flip as a locked
decision, test/identify.test.js additions.

Design (decided):
1. New boolean flag --eval-repro (default false -- the new safe default).
   Unset (default): sanitizeRepro unconditionally rejects the "node -e"
   shape (falls through to blind reread -- existing path; functional cost
   is one extra frontier verdict call per candidate that would previously
   have used eval repro). "node --test" and grep shapes remain allowed
   unconditionally -- they execute repo-authored code or no code at all, a
   different trust class than model-proposed "node -e". Set: v0.1 behavior
   (eval proposals sanitized and run).
2. Refresh: stored reproduction records of method:"command" whose cmd is
   eval-shaped are re-executed ONLY when --eval-repro is passed to refresh;
   otherwise the finding is flagged stale-unverifiable with a declared
   reason. This is the INVERSE of v0.1's default -- call it out explicitly
   in the T8 SPEC addendum as a behavior change, not just a new flag.
3. Record the flag in state.json run history (beginRun already records
   command/provider; add a flags snapshot if trivially additive, else skip
   -- the declared-degradation note in gate records is the requirement).

Acceptance criteria:
- AC1: Without the flag (default), a proposed "node -e" repro is never
  spawned (assert via exec-spy) and the candidate proceeds to blind
  reread. (Integration test.)
- AC2: With --eval-repro, behavior matches v0.1. Note: T0 rails stay green
  under this AC ONLY because T0's non-pin rule forbids pinning exact
  pass/verified counts -- those numbers can legitimately differ once eval
  repro is opted back in. (Integration test.)
- AC3: Refresh without the flag flags eval-repro findings as unverifiable
  with a reason, exit code unchanged; with the flag, re-executes them as
  v0.1 did. (Integration test, both branches.)
- AC4: Help/README/SKILL.md updated; README states the default explicitly
  and cites the security rationale. (Spawn test asserts the flag and its
  default appear in help output.)

Full context: handoff.md §3 "T7".

tickets.title: Docs, spec addendum, version bump, and prosecution (v0.2.0 close-out)
tickets.body:
Motivation: close the loop; keep SPEC.md as law. This is the
final ticket -- every other ticket must have merged to main before this one
runs, and it carries the release-facing documentation and the pre-merge
adversarial-review prosecution gate for the whole v0.2.0 diff.

Scope: SPEC.md (addendum section "v0.2.0 -- D2 hardening", recording the
new locked decisions: packetized coverage, persisted per-cell dry state,
charter-weighted pool width via --n, semantic dedupe fail-open rationale,
NEED_CONTEXT bound, distill phase, eval-repro flipped to safe-by-default --
call this last one out explicitly as a behavior change from v0.1, not just
a new flag), README.md final pass (including the "Sizing --budget" table
from T1 AC9), package.json version -> 0.2.0, CHANGELOG section in README or
new CHANGELOG.md.

Acceptance criteria:
- AC1: SPEC addendum documents every behavior change shipped, in the
  existing D#-style locked-decision format.
- AC2: node --test fully green; npm pack --dry-run includes no new
  unintended files.
- AC3: PROSECUTION PASS: adversarial review of the full diff (npx
  adversarial-review --base main, exit 0) -- run loop-until-dry per the
  house rule; all CRITICAL/HIGH findings resolved. Pass evidence recorded
  via adlc prosecute (two consecutive dry passes required); ledger sealed
  with adlc gate-manifest verify.
- AC4: DISTILL THE BUILD ITSELF: run lesson-foundry over this build's own
  verified review findings and skill-rot over do-better/references/*
  (stamps last-verified after the T1-T4 doc changes). Output committed or
  explicitly declared empty.

Full context: handoff.md entire document is this ticket's scope of review;
§2b for the mechanical gate discipline (pre-merge check vs post-merge
record split), §6 for the full verification protocol, §7 for known risks
to re-check before close-out.
